From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [John Randall Reding](/w/index.php?title=John_Randall_Reding&redirect=no "John Randall Reding"))

American politician

**John Randall Reding** (October 18, 1805 – October 8, 1892) was an American politician who served as a [U.S. Representative](/wiki/United_States_House_of_Representatives "United States House of Representatives") from [New Hampshire](/wiki/New_Hampshire "New Hampshire").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_R._Reding&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Born in [Portsmouth, New Hampshire](/wiki/Portsmouth,_New_Hampshire "Portsmouth, New Hampshire"), Reding attended public schools. He was apprenticed to the printer's trade and subsequently became a newspaper editor.

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_R._Reding&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

Reding was elected as a [Democrat](/wiki/Democratic_Party_(United_States) "Democratic Party (United States)") to the [27th](/wiki/27th_United_States_Congress "27th United States Congress") and [28th United States Congress](/wiki/28th_United_States_Congress "28th United States Congress"). After leaving congress, he served as naval storekeeper at Portsmouth from 1853 to 1858, and as mayor of Portsmouth in 1860. He was later a member of the [New Hampshire House of Representatives](/wiki/New_Hampshire_House_of_Representatives "New Hampshire House of Representatives") from 1867 to 1870.

## Death

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_R._Reding&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Death")]

Reding died in Portsmouth on October 8, 1892, and was interred in Haverhill Cemetery in [Haverhill, New Hampshire](/wiki/Haverhill,_New_Hampshire "Haverhill, New Hampshire").

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_R._Reding&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: See also")]

* [List of mayors of Portsmouth, New Hampshire](/wiki/List_of_mayors_of_Portsmouth,_New_Hampshire "List of mayors of Portsmouth, New Hampshire")
